rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: haudu, dinaa beligge ondu maili naditiini, niivu eenu maadtiiri?
rows.english: Yes, I walk a mile in the morning. everyday. What do you do ?

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: I will just stay at home.

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: If so don't you feel bored ?

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: asteenuu illa, beligge beere kelsa iratte.
rows.english: Not so much, I will have some other work in the morning.
I stroll about in the evening.

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: laalbaag varege hoogtiini, alli aidu nimisa niltiini athvaa hullin meele kuudtiini.
rows.english: I go upto Lalbagh, there I stop for five minutes or sit on the grass.

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: At what time you come back ?

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: entu gantege vaapas bartiini. aaga nim maneeli nim hendti obbareirtaara ?
rows.english: I come back at eight O' clock. Will your wife be alone at home at that time ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: No, the maid servant will be with her.
